features.summary: P&G reported strong fiscal 2021 results with organic sales growth of 6% and core EPS growth of 11%. The company announced leadership changes with Jon Moeller becoming CEO in November and David Taylor remaining as Executive Chair. Q4 organic sales grew 4% despite tough comparisons, with strong market share gains across categories. The company faces significant commodity and freight cost headwinds of $1.9 billion in fiscal 2022 but is implementing price increases and productivity measures to offset these pressures.
features.management_outlook: Management expects fiscal 2022 organic sales growth of 2-4% and core EPS growth of 3-6%, with stronger performance in the back half due to easier comparisons. The company plans to invest $15-17 billion in shareholder returns through dividends and share repurchases. Leadership emphasized continued investment in innovation and marketing despite cost pressures, viewing current challenges as temporary. They remain confident in their strategic framework of superiority, productivity, and constructive disruption to drive long-term growth.
features.risks: The primary risk is the $1.9 billion after-tax headwind from commodity costs (up 30-200%) and freight expenses ($100 million impact) in fiscal 2022. These cost pressures are heavily weighted toward the first half of the year. Additional risks include continued COVID-19 volatility, potential currency weakness, geopolitical disruption, and competitive pressures in key categories like Baby Care. The company also faces challenging year-over-year comparisons in major markets like the US and China.
features.key_drivers: Strong innovation pipeline and superior product performance continue driving market share gains, with US all-outlet value share reaching 34% (highest in 20 years). E-commerce sales grew 35% to over $10 billion, representing 14% of total sales. The company is implementing strategic price increases across multiple categories and markets to offset cost inflation. Productivity improvements of 320 basis points in Q4 demonstrate the company's ability to fund investments while managing margin pressure through operational efficiency.
